articles.title: axios Package Compromised; Malicious Versions Added a Trojan Dependency
articles.description: Axios is an HTTP library that gets 100M+ downloads a week, largely due to its legacy popularity. An attacker took advantage of that to roll out a version with a malicious dependency including a remote access trojan (though Axios' codebase itself was fine). This is big, as even if you don’t use Axios, your dependencies might. Here's how to see if you're affected.
articles.author: Ashish Kurmi

articles.title: Transformers.js v4: Run AI Models in the Browser
articles.description: Brings Hugging Face-hosted transformer models into JavaScript, so you can run NLP, vision, and audio models in-browser. v4 switches to a WebGPU runtime and is installable with npm. There are many live demos covering real-time speech transcription, using Qwen 3.5, and real-time video captioning.
articles.author: Hugging Face

articles.title: Signals: The Push-Pull Based Algorithm
articles.description: A well-diagrammed ground-up explanation of how signals work internally, focusing on the push-pull algorithm at the core of reactivity in frameworks like Solid, Vue, and Angular.
articles.author: Willy Brauner

articles.title: 🖼️ Your Options for Preloading Images with JavaScript
articles.description: “There are a number of ways to preload an image on demand with JavaScript, each with their own strengths and drawbacks. Let's explore them.”
articles.author: Alex MacArthur

articles.title: A Gentle Intro to npm Workspaces (With Visuals)
articles.description: Workspaces let you manage multiple packages in one repo and link local packages so they can import each other by name. npm may then hoist and deduplicate compatible dependencies during install.
articles.author: Carlos Precioso

articles.title: Pretext: A Multiline Text Measurement and Layout Library
articles.description: Cheng Lou, formerly a React core team member, caused a stir with this X post three days ago, racking up 22M impressions and getting 25k stars on this repo since. Why? People are very excited about the potential for real time web layouts! There are demos here if you want to see what the excitement is about, although the library itself is reasonably straightforward.
articles.author: Cheng Lou

articles.title: Knip v6: The Tool to Declutter Your JS/TS Projects
articles.description: Knip is a go-to tool for finding and removing unused files, exports, and dependencies in projects. v6 integrates oxc for 2-4x performance gains (it tears through Astro in two seconds) and is largely a drop-in upgrade.
articles.author: Lars Kappert

articles.title: 📺 ArtPlayer: A Modern, Full-Featured HTML5 Video Player
articles.description: A straightforward way to get your own heavily-customizable YouTube-style player experience. There’s a full, live demo/playground showing it off.
articles.author: Harvey Zhao

articles.title: Semiotic 3.0: React + D3 Data Visualization Framework
articles.description: Does the basics well, but has some more unique offerings like choropleth maps, Sankey diagrams, flow maps, and violin plots, plus streaming data support.
articles.author: nteract

articles.description: Inertia.js 3.0 – Glue between React, Vue and Svelte SPAs and non-JS server-side frameworks like Laravel, Rails and Django. More on v3 here.
Node.js March 24, 2026 Security Releases - Including Node.js v25.8.2 (Current), v24.14.1 (LTS), v22.22.2 (LTS), and v20.20.2 (LTS).
TanStack DB 0.6 – Now with persistence, offline support, and hierarchical data.
Astro 6.1, Mantine 9.0, Ky 2.0 Prerelease, CKEditor 48.0, pnpm 10.33

articles.title: Pretext: A Multiline Text Measurement and Layout Library
articles.description: This caused quite the stir this week, with some people even saying it'll redefine how layouts are made on the Web. Check out this dynamic layout demo for a taste. Essentially it opens the door to real-time, multiline text measurement and dynamic layouts, without touching the DOM. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Cheng Lou

articles.title: What is CSS Containment and How Can I Use It?
articles.description: Harry takes a look at this “woefully underused” feature, explaining what the CSS contain property actually is, what each of its values does, and how to use it confidently without creating hard-to-debug side effects.
articles.author: Harry Roberts

articles.title: Your Options for Preloading Images with JavaScript
articles.description: There's several ways to approach this, and, as Alex notes it can be “surprisingly quirky [..] and the best one to choose can very much depend on the circumstances.”
articles.author: Alex MacArthur

articles.title: Applying Accessibility Fixes with Stealth for the Greater Good
articles.description: Advice on how to bake accessibility into your daily workflow and break down any systematic roadblocks, in the hope that a11y considerations become an inherent part of your process, rather than an afterthought.
articles.author: Steve Frenzel

articles.title: 🍪 Cookie Consent Management in 2026
articles.description: Jason notes that more and more “companies are trying to get their cookie house in order” — as such, he shares some high-level thoughts on what to be thinking about, along with some more technical advice here.
articles.author: Jason Grigsby

articles.title: Magic Link Pitfalls
articles.description: The increasingly popular login pattern (I’m not a fan) has some security issues to be aware of. Evan shares those here, and also came back, having found some more .
articles.author: Evan Todd

articles.title: Preserving the Web is Not The Problem. Losing It Is
articles.description: Some news organizations are restricting access to the Wayback Machine over fears of AI scraping. Mark Graham explains why these concerns are unfounded.
articles.author: Mark Graham

articles.title: Announcing Babylon.js 9.0
articles.description: A major release of Microsoft's popular rendering engine for building interactive, 3D web experiences packing in “a year of new features, optimizations, and performance improvements”, including a new clustered lighting system, a node-based particle editor, volumetric lighting, advanced Gaussian Splatting, and more. Repo here.
articles.author: Carter & Lucchini (Microsoft)

articles.title: Web Platform Features Explorer: Discover New Web Platform Features and APIs
articles.description: A useful way to keep up with what’s new on the web platform (including an RSS option). You can filter by newly available, widely available, by browser, etc. The data is pulled from the W3C WebDX Community Group repos. These monthly release notes are particularly valuable.
articles.author: WebDX CG

articles.title: 🤖 Transformers.js v4: Run AI Models in the Browser
articles.description: Brings Hugging Face-hosted transformer models into the browser, meaning you can run numerous vision, audio and NLP models right in the page. This new version switches to a WebGPU runtime and is installable via npm. Live demos.
articles.author: Hugging Face

articles.title: Heerich.js: A Tiny Engine for 3D Voxel Scenes Rendered to SVG
articles.description: A minimalist JavaScript engine that constructs 3D compositions and distills them into ‘pristine’ SVGs. Pure vector output, zero dependencies. This is a really well presented site which runs through what it can do, and how.
articles.author: David Aerne

articles.title: XML Comparator: A Web Tool to Compare XML Files
articles.description: Shows you the original, modified, and a full diffed version that allows you to select if you want to ignore whitespace or fold matched lines for easier diffing.
articles.author: XML Comparator

articles.title: CSS is DOOMed: Rendering DOOM in 3D with CSS
articles.description: Every single wall, barrel, and imp is a div positioned in 3D space using CSS transform. The logic is JS, but all of the rendering is entirely CSS. Give it a try here.
articles.author: Niels Leenheer
